The current is churning with jagged debris from the surface cities, shredding the delicate coral gardens.
I must swim deeper into the abyss to escape the thunderous shockwaves of the shifting tectonic plates.
The water has turned a suffocating grey with ash and silt, blinding my sight and stinging my gills.
My pod is scattered across the reef as the massive surge of water pulls us toward the open ocean.
I can feel the seabed trembling beneath my tail, signaling a great upheaval that will reshape our caverns.
The surface water is shimmering with a violet light that makes the currents feel heavy and strange.
I have never seen the stars dance in such erratic patterns through the shimmering mirror of the tide.
My scales are humming in resonance with the pulsing glow descending from the dark void above.
The deep pressure of the trench feels safer than this eerie radiance bleeding into the upper shallows.
I wonder if the great whales can feel the pull of that falling fire against their ancient songs.
Your current reef markers drift too far into the warm current where my pod hunts.
The shimmering sands of this trench have belonged to my sisters since the great migration.
I will not allow your schools of guards to patrol the vents of my grotto.
Move your boundary stones three leagues east or feel the crush of the deep tide.
This coral spire is my sanctuary, and your bubble-dwellers must stop encroaching upon its base.
The current pulls strangely toward this shimmering rift, dragging the salt from my gills.
I wonder if the water on the other side is as clear as the sapphire depths of my home.
My scales prickle with a cold energy that does not belong in the warm currents of the reef.
If I swim through this glowing veil, will my tail still propel me through the brine?
This swirling vortex tastes of ozone and ancient metal, unlike any current I have ever chased.
My scales are drying and itching against these coarse, scratching ferns and thorns.
I must drag my heavy tail through the mud to find a stream.
The air feels thin and searing compared to the cool weight of the ocean.
Every inch of this suffocating greenery keeps me far from the salt spray.
I can only hope these tangled roots lead me toward a deep pool.
The currents have carried every singing voice away from these frozen depths.
My gills ache from the stillness of a sea that no longer speaks.
I drift through the kelp forests waiting for a single vibration to break this void.
The silence is a heavy pressure that weighs more than the deepest ocean trench.
I have forgotten the melody of my own kind in this endless, quiet blue.
You will find no footing in these depths to strike me with your iron.
I shall drag you down into the crushing black where your lungs will burst.
The currents obey my call and will sweep you against the jagged reef walls.
My sisters are circling in the foam and they hunger for your sinking soul.
Try to pierce my skin and I will pull you into the silent abyss.
This iridescent nautilus shell reflects the deep currents and holds the echoes of the abyss.
I guard this polished obsidian mirror because it captures the flickering light of the surface.
My favorite treasure is a heavy gold chain tangled with salt-crusted gems from a sunken galley.
No other creature in the reef possesses a pearl as perfectly spherical and luminous as this.
I cherish this silver fork, a strange and gleaming relic from the world above the waves.
The warm current is receding, and I must hoard the remaining heat for my eggs.
These few remaining tide pools are all that sustain the fry during the low ebb.
I can only dive to the deep vents a handful of times before my lungs ache.
The shimmering schools of silverfish are dwindling, leaving us to fight over the smallest scraps.
Only one shell of salt remains in the grotto to keep my scales from drying.
